Clinical Associate Professor, Touro DOB: 8/21/51 University Phone #: 702.523.1109 Cyndi Tran, D.O. Primary Insurance: Medicare Consultant Neurologist Secondary Insurance AARP Adrienne Pan, D.O. Fellowship Trained in Behavioral Neurology and Refer to: A Plus Home Health Neuropsychiatry Specialty: Address: Diagnostic Testing Cognitive Testing Phone #: 702.720.7929 DBS Interrogation Fax #: 702.342.8398 EEG- Ambulatory Reason for Referral: OT FOR Hand / finger stiffness EEG- Routine and Lumbar radiculpathy to RLE DX: G64, M20.10, M20.40, M47 816, G56.00 EEG- Video Ambulatory Nerve Conduction Studies/EMG Physician Signature: VNS Interrogation Office Location Please send back your input and work up details to us. Las Vegas, NV 89148 Phone: (702) 732-2600 X Dr. Venkat Veerappan, MD Dr. Adrienne Pan, DO Fax: (702) 732-2622 From:Desert Neurology 702 732 2622 02/11/2026 08:30 #549 P.002/005 Desert Neurology 9280 W Sunset Road Suite 236 Las Vegas, NV 89148 Phone: (702) 732-2600 Fax: (702) 732-2622 February 11, 2026 Available Demographic Information PATIENT GLORIAALLEN (DOB: 8/21/1951, F) SS #: 530424391 ID: 23282 8408 TURTLE CREEK CIRCLE LAS VEGAS, NV 89113 (702) 523-1109 Cell: (702) 523-1109 OTHER Race: Unknown Ethnicity: Unknown Language Preference: English INSURANCE INFORMATION Medicare of Nevada - J1 / Medicare of Nevada - J1 (Primary) ID #: 7RN0CF9VC39 Group #: Usual Copay: 0.00 TX, - (701) 277-6784 Notes: 1/26/26 - active thru cmd - cr AARP- United Health Care Plans / AARP- United Health Care Plans (Secondary) ID #: 33881581611 Group #: Usual Copay: 0.00 PO BOX 1600 KINGSTON, NY, 12402 Notes: 1/26/26 - active thru cmd - cr Printed By: Mayra Reyes, FRONT DESK ASSISTANT 2/11/2026 8:22:17 AM Amazing Charts Page 1 of 1 The information on this page is CONFIDENTIAL Any release of this information requires the expressed written authorization of the patient listed above. From:Desert Neurology 702 732 2622 02/11/2026 08:30 #549 P.003/005 Desert Neurology 9280 W Sunset Road Suite 236 Las Vegas, NV 89148 Phone (702) 732-2600 Fax (702) 732-2622 ALLEN, GLORIA (DOB: 8/21/1951 ID: 23282) F Jan 26, 2026 Mon 08:52 AM CC Patient is here to follow up on her neuropathy. HPI 01/26/26- Patient was last seen in office 09/23/2024 by Venkat Veerappan for neuropathy. She is following up today on her neuropathy. She said that she wants to talk about what can be recommended for her nerves. She said that she notices on her right side and it pinches in her lower back and she doesn't want to have surgery. She said that shes trying PT and exercise. She said that she has noticed that the nerves on the right side hands and feet aren't great. She said that she doesn't like medications. She doesn't like the blood pressure machine and it gives her anxiety. She said that she runs 4 times a week in cali and its 4.82 miles and that she does yoga and weights. Pt is interested in getting medicine for her numbness/tingling. She has been staying active, she runs, does pilates, and yoga. Dr. Duke wants for her to have surgery however she is trying to hold off on having surgery. Pt complains of tingling in the sole of her right foot. This is intermittent in nature. Pt also mentions that her hands sometimes when she is doing weights her fingers get stuck. After doing some massages it goes away. This all started around last year however it has been worsening recently. Pt denies experiencing any weakness on her hands, denies dropping any objects. Pt denies any numbness/tingling anywhere else. Pt also denies any neck pain. Tingliness is not severe enough for her to want to take medicine. Low back pain is exacerbated when she is resting. Pain radiates down her RLE. This is intermittent in nature. ROS Endorses low back pain and right buttuck pain Endorses numbness and tingling in the RLE Denies any falls or gait disturbances PMH Back pain Cervical spondylosis Lumbar spondylosis Endocrine: Menopausal or Post-Menopausal SH [Tobacco: Never smoker] ETOH glass of wine social Right handed FH Structured Family History Father deceased at age 102, no medical issues Sister: Breast cancer Allergies 1) Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics) Meds (Reconciled by VENKAT on 1/26/2026 8:59:27 AM) Printed By: Mayra Reyes, FRONT DESK ASSISTANT 2/11/2026 8:22:26 AM Amazing Charts Page 1 of 3 The information on this page is confidential. Any release of this information requires the written authorization of the patient listed above. From:Desert Neurology 702 732 2622 02/11/2026 08:31 #549 P.004/005 ALLEN, GLORIA (DOB: 8/21/1951 ID: 23282) F Jan 26, 2026 Mon 08:52 AM 1) Celebrex 100 mg capsule 2) cranberry 3) cyclop 4) estradiol 5) magnesium, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 6) Probiotic 7) Prolia 60 mg/mL subcutaneous syringe 8) tramadol 50 mg tablet 9) Vitmin D 10) Zinc Vitals Wt: 90.6 lb Ht/Ln: 62 in BMI: 16.6 BP: 134/88 Pulse: 84 Temp: 98.0F PE Pt is awake, alert & oriented X3. NAD. Speech, Cognition, Mood & Affect are Within Normal Limits. Cranial Nerves I-XII: Sense of smell, taste & hearing to fingers rubbing is intact. Head/Neck rotation and shoulder shrugging is intact B/L & symmetrical. Motor: Bulk & tone normal throughout. Power is 5/5 & symmetrical in both upper & lower extremities proximally & distally. Reflexes: Deep Tendon Reflexes are 3/4 at biceps, triceps, brachioradialis, patellae & achilles. Plantars are downgoing B/L. Sensory: Light touch, pinprick, cold, position & vibration sensation is intact throughout & symmetrical. Cerebellar: Finger-nose-finger & Heel-knee-shin exams are normal. Rapid alternating movements are normal. Romberg sign: Negative. Gait: Casual gait is normal. Tandem gait is normal. Heel & toe walking is normal. Extremities: Hands: L index finger: heberdens nodule. R foot: hallux valgus, dorsal muscle atrophy. L foot: hallux valgus, dorsal muscle atrophy, hammer toes digits 2-5. Positive Hoffma's on right hand Carpal compression test bilateral negative A/P # Peripheral neuropathy (G64): B/L UE and LE # Hallux valgus (M20.10): # Hammer toe (M20.40): # Lumbar spondylosis (M47.816): # Carpal tunnel (G56.00): B/L Plan printed and provided to patient: ADDED Current Meds: magnesium, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily, # 0, RF: 0. Dr.Anthony Ricciardi - Foot Surgeon - surgery was planned for Jan 2025 however she refused On Celebrex 100mg po daily prn from PCP Refer to occupational therapy for hand fingers stiffness (fingers getting stuck) and lumbar radiculpathy to her RLE Will repeat EMG/NCS of BUE to see if there is more nerve damage Follow up in 3 months Elia Barraza MD Neurology Resident, PGY-3 PROVIDED: Patient Education (1/26/2026) Printed By: Mayra Reyes, FRONT DESK ASSISTANT 2/11/2026 8:22:26 AM Amazing Charts Page 2 of 3 The information on this page is confidential. Any release of this information requires the written authorization of the patient listed above.
